---
title: "Sydney Airbnb Pricing"
output:
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: fill
    theme: cosmo
    favicon: null
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(flexdashboard)
library(tidyverse)
library(leaflet)
library(DT)
library(broom)
library(scales)

set.seed(42)

ACCENT  <- "#FF5A5F"   # Inside Airbnb pink (overpriced / accent)
NEUTRAL <- "#484848"   # dark grey
BLUE    <- "#2C7BB6"   # underpriced

theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
            theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank()))

df  <- readRDS("airbnb_clean.rds")
mod <- readRDS("model.rds")   # preferred model (M2, additive)
kn  <- readRDS("key_numbers.rds")
```

Row {data-height=110}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Executive summary

```{r summary, results='asis'}
cat(sprintf(
"Using **%s active Sydney Airbnb listings** from the Inside Airbnb scrape of **%s**,
an additive log-linear regression of nightly price on size, room type, location
and review signals achieves an **adjusted R&sup2; of %.1f%%**. Size dominates —
each extra bathroom adds about **+%.0f%%** and each bedroom **+%.0f%%** — while
superhosts earn a **+%.0f%%** premium. Geography is captured by suburb, not raw
distance: **%s** listings sit about **%.0f%% above** what features alone predict,
whereas **%s** sits roughly **%.0f%% below**. The map and tables surface where
individual listings are priced **below** their comparable set (blue, potentially
underpriced) versus **above** (red).",
  comma(kn$n_listings), kn$scrape_date, kn$adj_r2,
  kn$bathroom_pct, kn$bedroom_pct, kn$superhost_pct,
  kn$top_over_region, kn$top_over_pct,
  kn$top_under_region, abs(kn$top_under_pct)))
```

Row {data-height=520}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Where the mispricing is &nbsp; <span style="color:#2C7BB6">&#9679; under</span> &nbsp; <span style="color:#FF5A5F">&#9679; over</span> &nbsp;(model residual)

```{r map}
map_df <- df %>% slice_sample(n = min(2000, nrow(df)))

# Diverging palette centred on zero, capped at symmetric quantiles so a few
# extreme residuals don't wash out the colour scale.
lim <- quantile(abs(df$residual), 0.95)
pal <- colorNumeric(c(BLUE, "#f7f7f7", ACCENT),
                    domain = c(-lim, lim))

leaflet(map_df) %>%
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") %>%
  setView(lng = 151.2093, lat = -33.8688, zoom = 11) %>%
  addCircleMarkers(
    ~longitude, ~latitude,
    radius = 4, stroke = FALSE, fillOpacity = 0.7,
    color = ~pal(pmax(pmin(residual, lim), -lim)),
    label = ~sprintf("%s, %s: $%s (model $%s)",
                     room_type, neighbourhood_group,
                     comma(round(price)), comma(round(predicted_price)))
  ) %>%
  addLegend("bottomright", pal = pal, values = c(-lim, lim),
            title = "Residual<br>(log price)", opacity = 0.9)
```

### Most mispriced listings

```{r tables}
mk <- function(d) {
  d %>%
    transmute(Region = neighbourhood_group,
              `Room type` = room_type,
              Price = dollar(round(price)),
              `Model price` = dollar(round(predicted_price)),
              `% vs model` = round(pct_vs_model)) %>%
    datatable(rownames = FALSE, options = list(dom = "t", pageLength = 10),
              class = "compact stripe") %>%
    formatStyle("% vs model",
                color = styleInterval(0, c(BLUE, ACCENT)),
                fontWeight = "bold")
}

under <- df %>% arrange(residual) %>% slice_head(n = 10)
over  <- df %>% arrange(desc(residual)) %>% slice_head(n = 10)
```

#### {.tabset}

##### Underpriced (raise price)

```{r}
mk(under)
```

##### Overpriced (model missing features)

```{r}
mk(over)
```

Row {data-height=440}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### What drives nightly price (main effects, % per unit, 95% CI)

```{r coef-plot}
tidy(mod, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(across(c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high), ~ (exp(.) - 1) * 100),
         term = term %>%
           str_replace("room_type", "Room: ") %>%
           str_replace("neighbourhood_group", "Region: ") %>%
           str_replace("host_is_superhostTRUE", "Superhost") %>%
           str_replace("dist_to_cbd_km", "Per km from CBD") %>%
           str_replace("review_scores_rating", "Per review-score point"),
         term = fct_reorder(term, abs(estimate))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(estimate, term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                  colour = NEUTRAL, fatten = 2) +
  labs(x = "Effect on nightly price (%)", y = NULL)
```

### Suburb premium vs what features alone predict

```{r region-premium-plot}
# How much each region sits above/below a features-only model — the real
# geographic story (raw distance-to-CBD is not significant once suburb is known).
kn$region_premium %>%
  mutate(neighbourhood_group = fct_reorder(neighbourhood_group, premium_pct),
         sign = if_else(premium_pct >= 0, "above", "below")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(premium_pct, neighbourhood_group, fill = sign)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey60") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(above = ACCENT, below = BLUE), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  labs(x = "Average premium vs features-only model", y = NULL)
```
